data.content: Now I have the full picture. Let me create comprehensive MSTest tests for `OrderService`.
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. The user wants MSTest unit tests for the `OrderService` class. Let me also check the legacy-antipatterns to see what patterns to avoid, and the service-registry for any additional context.

Let me write comprehensive tests following MSTest best practices:
1. Sealed test classes
2. `Assert.ThrowsExactly` (MSTest 3.x)
3. Good test naming conventions
4. Proper test organization

toolCalls.name: create

data.content: Now let me verify the tests build and pass:

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-c9c73fe460b9418aaa93895171984d33/fixtures/modern-mstest && dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -20", "description": "Build and run MSTest tests", "initial_wait": 60}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-c9c73fe460b9418aaa93895171984d33/fixtures/modern-mstest && dotnet test 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with full output", "initial_wait": 60}
